headline: Limits and Frontiers of Quantum Information
summary: Today's research spans the theoretical and practical limits of quantum systems. While some studies challenge the existence of quantum advantage in optimization, others push the boundaries of quantum thermodynamics, secure key distribution, and mathematical foundations of quantum information.

themes.title: Security and Randomness in Quantum Networks
themes.body: New protocols leverage high-dimensional quantum states and measurement incompatibility to secure communication. Researchers demonstrated robust 11-dimensional quantum key distribution and established tight bounds on eavesdropping using steering and incompatibility witnesses.

themes.title: Limits of Quantum Advantage and Dynamics
themes.body: Semiclassical approximations cast doubt on quantum advantage for spin glass optimization, showing classical models can match or beat QAOA. Meanwhile, a new geometric framework helps decode complex quantum dynamics by isolating redundant data in higher-order correlators.

themes.title: Thermodynamics and Information Theory
themes.body: Developments in quantum thermodynamics and mathematical physics introduce a superconducting heat engine that approaches Carnot efficiency at finite power, alongside a unified theory extending quantum hockey stick f-divergences to infinite dimensions.

connections.body: Both papers utilize quantum steering and measurement-based security bounds to limit the capabilities of potential eavesdroppers, linking high-dimensional physical implementations to fundamental randomness certification.
